title: 10 best herbal teas for heartburn
intro_desc: Herbal teas for heartburn are caffeine-free infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices, which can provide relief from the discomfort of heartburn. They help with heartburn by reducing inflammation, promoting digestion, and neutralizing stomach acid. Some popular herbal teas for heartburn include chamomile, ginger, marshmallow root, and licorice root teas. These teas can be enjoyed either hot or cold and make a soothing alternative to other beverages. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their heartburn symptoms, ultimately enhancing their overall quality of life and enabling them to enjoy their meals without worry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with heartburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from heartburn symptoms. Its bitter properties can also stimulate digestive function, increasing the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which can help break down food more efficiently and prevent acid reflux. Additionally, yarrow tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. Drinking yarrow tea regularly may help prevent heartburn and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from heartburn.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, resulting from tannins, help constrict and tighten the tissues in the digestive tract, reducing acid reflux and alleviating heartburn.

Carminative: Yarrow tea's carminative properties, owing to volatile oils like chamazulene, can help expel excess gas from the digestive tract, preventing bloating and discomfort associated with heartburn.

Antispasmodic: Yarrow tea's antispasmodic properties, due to lactones and flavonoids, can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can worsen heartburn.

Bitter tonic: The bitter principles in yarrow tea, such as lactones and flavonoids, can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, improving overall digestion and reducing the likelihood of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the yarrow and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your heartburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in flavonoids, making them helpful in reducing inflammation and providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds contain essential oils that can aid in digestion and alleviate heartburn discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with heartburn because it contains bitter compounds that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which can improve overall digestion and help reduce heartburn symptoms. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, providing additional relief. Furthermore, wormwood has been shown to have a calming effect on the gastrointestinal system, which can help prevent acid reflux and alleviate discomfort caused by heartburn. Drinking wormwood tea regularly may help promote a healthy digestive system and reduce the frequency and severity of heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tea helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate the symptoms of heartburn by decreasing irritation and promoting healing. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Cholagogue: Wormwood tea stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and can help prevent the formation of stomach acid that leads to heartburn. The active constituents responsible for this property include absinthin and anabsinthin.

Carminative: Wormwood tea helps to expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort that can exacerbate heartburn. The volatile oils present in wormwood, such as thujone and pinene, contribute to this carminative effect.

Antimicrobial: Wormwood tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which can help prevent infections that can trigger heartburn. The active constituents responsible for this property include alpha- and beta-thujone.

Spasmolytic: Wormwood tea helps to relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to heartburn symptoms. This is due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried wormwood herb to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, then strain the tea into a cup, discarding the wormwood herb.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature, which may take 5-10 minutes.
Drink the tea slowly, sipping it gently, to help soothe heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Wormwood leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for heartburn due to their bitter properties which can help stimulate digestion and reduce symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of wormwood contain volatile oils and other compounds that have been shown to have antispasmodic effects, which can help relieve heartburn caused by muscle spasms in the digestive tract.

Seeds: Wormwood seeds are rich in essential oils and have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including heartburn, due to their carminative properties which can help reduce bloating and gas.

Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of wormwood can still contribute to the medicinal properties of teas for heartburn, as they contain similar compounds that can help stimulate digestion and reduce symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch tea helps with heartburn because it contains betulinic acid, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties. This acid can help soothe the inflammation in the esophagus caused by heartburn. Additionally, silver birch tea is a natural diuretic, which can help eliminate excess stomach acid and reduce the symptoms of heartburn. The tea is also caffeine-free, making it a gentler option for those who find that caffeinated beverages exacerbate their heartburn symptoms. Furthermore, silver birch tea has been shown to stimulate digestion, which can help prevent the buildup of stomach acid and alleviate heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Silver birch tea helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms, due to the presence of betulinic acid and other triterpenes.
Astringent: The tannins in silver birch tea help to tighten and strengthen tissue in the esophagus, reducing the risk of acid reflux, which can cause heartburn.
Diuretic: Silver birch tea promotes urination, helping to eliminate toxins and excess fluids from the body, which can contribute to heartburn, thanks to the flavonoids and saponins present.

Carminative: The tea can help prevent the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing bloating and discomfort, and minimizing heartburn, by containing volatile oils like limonene and pinene.

Antioxidant: Silver birch tea neutralizes free radicals in the body, protecting cells from damage and supporting overall digestive health, which helps reduce the occurrence of heartburn, due to its rich polyphenol content.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried silver birch leaves with 1 cup of cold water.
Slowly bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the silver birch tea daily, before or after meals, to help alleviate heartburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the silver birch plant are commonly used in making medicinal teas for heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract.
Bark: The bark of the silver birch plant contains compounds that can help stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Buds: The buds of the silver birch plant are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn.
Seeds: The seeds of the silver birch plant contain oils that can help soothe the digestive system and provide relief from heartburn symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with heartburn because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. It also contains an active compound called tannins, which can help neutralize stomach acids and reduce the symptoms of heartburn. Moreover, white tea is less processed than other teas, making it a more gentle and natural remedy for heartburn. Its soothing and calming effects can provide quick relief from the discomfort of acid reflux. Overall, white tea is a delicious and effective way to alleviate heartburn and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which is important for heartburn relief as inflammation can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the lining of the esophagus from damage caused by acid reflux, which is important for heartburn prevention. The main active constituents responsible for this property are polyphenols and catechins.

Astringent: White tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and strengthen the esophageal lining, which is important for preventing acid reflux and heartburn. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and catechins.

Gastroprotective: White tea has gastroprotective properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by stomach acid, which is important for heartburn prevention. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and saponins.

Anti-secretory: White tea has anti-secretory properties that can help reduce the production of stomach acid, which is important for heartburn prevention. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and alkaloids.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 160-185°F (71-85°C), as boiling water can damage the tea's delicate properties.
Add 1 teaspoon (2-3 grams) of dried organic white tea leaves to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let them steep for 1-5 minutes, depending on how strong you prefer the tea's flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used tea leaves.
Drink the herbal white tea slowly, sipping it gently to soothe your heartburn. Enjoy up to 3 cups of this tea per day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that can help relax the esophageal sphincter, preventing acid reflux and heartburn.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and discomfort in the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: Contain oils and compounds that can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing the risk of heartburn.
Rhyzomes: Have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help relax the digestive muscles and reduce the symptoms of heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis tea helps with heartburn because it contains cannabinoids, particularly tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) and cannabidiol (CBD), which interact with the endocannabinoid system in the human body. This system plays a vital role in regulating gastrointestinal functions, including acid production in the stomach. THC and CBD can help reduce gastric acid secretion, thus alleviating the symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of cannabinoids can help soothe inflamed esophageal tissue, providing further relief from heartburn discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cannabis tea can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for heartburn as it can help alleviate symptoms caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, such as THC and CBD, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system.

Analgesic: Cannabis tea has pain-relieving properties, which is important for heartburn as it can help reduce the discomfort and pain associated with the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, particularly THC, which binds to pain receptors in the brain and spinal cord.

Muscle relaxant: Cannabis tea can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which is important for heartburn as it can help reduce spasms and tension that can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, particularly CBD, which has been shown to have muscle relaxant properties.

Antiemetic: Cannabis tea can help reduce nausea and vomiting, which is important for heartburn as it can help alleviate symptoms caused by acid reflux. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, particularly THC, which interacts with the body's endocannabinoid system to reduce nausea and vomiting.

Antioxidant: Cannabis tea has antioxidant properties, which is important for heartburn as it can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and terpenes, which are found in the cannabis plant and have been shown to have antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Chop and combine 1 gram of cannabis flower, 1 tablespoon of crushed fennel seeds, and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers in a mortar and pestle.
Place the herbal mixture in a tea infuser and steep in a cup of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice for flavor.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, taking small sips every 10-15 minutes as needed to relieve heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in cannabinoids and terpenes, cannabis leaves can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from heartburn symptoms.
Flowers: High in CBD and other cannabinoids, cannabis flowers can help regulate gut motility and alleviate heartburn.
Seeds: Cannabis seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing heartburn relief.
Buds: Containing a variety of cannabinoids and terpenes, cannabis buds can help regulate digestion and reduce the symptoms of heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tea helps with heartburn because it contains capsaicin, a compound that can increase digestive fluid production and aid in digestion, thereby preventing acid reflux. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in antioxidants and vitamins, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract. The tea made from bell peppers can provide a natural and effective relief for heartburn symptoms, while also promoting overall gut health. Its mild flavor and soothing properties make it an excellent choice for those looking for a gentle and holistic approach to managing heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which is important for heartburn relief as inflammation can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include vitamin C, flavonoids, and carotenoids.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in bell pepper tea helps protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals, which is crucial for heartburn management as oxidative stress can contribute to heartburn symptoms. The primary antioxidants found in bell peppers are vitamin C, vitamin A, and flavonoids.

Mucilaginous: Bell pepper tea has a mucilaginous quality, which means it can form a protective layer on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract. This property is significant for heartburn treatment as it helps to prevent acid reflux and soothe irritated tissues.

Carminative: Bell pepper tea has carminative properties, which aid in reducing gastric inflammation and promoting the expulsion of excess gas from the gastrointestinal tract. This is important for heartburn relief as excessive gas can increase abdominal pressure, leading to acid reflux.

Diaphoretic: Bell pepper tea has diaphoretic properties, which stimulate perspiration and help to regulate body temperature. This property is beneficial for heartburn management as increased perspiration can assist in eliminating toxins from the body and reducing the overall acidity in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: In a mortar and pestle, grind one teaspoon each of chamomile flowers, marshmallow root, and cayenne pepper, taking about 5 minutes.
Finely dice one bell pepper and add it to the ground herbs.
In a teapot, combine the herb-pepper mixture with four cups of boiling water, letting it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid remnants.
Consume the herbal bell pepper tea throughout the day, as needed for heartburn relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from heartburn.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate heartburn.
Flowers: Contain compounds that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing symptoms of heartburn.
Stems: May have antispasmodic properties that can help relieve muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing heartburn symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shagbark hickory tea helps with heartburn because it contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Additionally, the tea is known to stimulate digestion and soothe the gastrointestinal tract, which can help prevent acid reflux and reduce the production of stomach acid. Furthermore, shagbark hickory tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and natural alternative to over-the-counter heartburn remedies. Its pleasant taste and soothing effects make it an excellent choice for those seeking a natural and effective way to manage heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Shagbark hickory tea contains tannins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Astringent: The astringent properties of shagbark hickory tea, due to its tannin content, help constrict and tighten the tissues in the esophagus, reducing acid reflux and heartburn.
Antispasmodic: The tea's antispasmodic properties, attributed to its volatile oils, help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing spasms and easing heartburn.

Demulcent: Shagbark hickory tea forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, thanks to its mucilaginous polysaccharides, alleviating irritation and discomfort caused by heartburn.
Acid-neutralizing: The tea's ability to neutralize stomach acid, due to its alkaloid content, aids in balancing the pH levels in the stomach, thereby reducing the occurrence of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 shagbark hickory bark pieces (each about 8 inches long) and rinse them thoroughly.
Chop the bark into small pieces, place them in a pot, and add 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the bark pieces.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed for heartburn relief.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the shagbark hickory tree contains tannins, which can help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from heartburn.
Leaves: The leaves of the shagbark hickory plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe heartburn.
Seeds: The seeds of the shagbark hickory tree contain high levels of magnesium, which can help to regulate stomach acid and provide relief from heartburn.
Flowers: The flowers of the shagbark hickory plant contain antioxidants that can help to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, providing relief from heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tea helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms. Additionally, gotu kola has been shown to improve digestion and reduce gastric acid secretion, which can help prevent the acid reflux that causes heartburn. Furthermore, the tea has calming effects that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to heartburn. Drinking gotu kola tea regularly may help alleviate heartburn symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tea can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for heartburn as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort. This property is attributed to the triterpenoid saponins present in gotu kola.

Mucoprotective: Gotu kola tea can protect and strengthen the mucous membrane lining the digestive tract, which is crucial for heartburn as it can help prevent acid reflux and damage to the esophagus. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins in gotu kola.

Antispasmodic: Gotu kola tea has the ability to relax smooth muscles, which is essential for heartburn as it can help reduce spasms and tension in the gastrointestinal tract, allowing for better digestion and reduced symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of essential oils and alkaloids in gotu kola.

Digestive stimulant: Gotu kola tea can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which is important for heartburn as it can improve digestion and nutrient absorption, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and heartburn symptoms. This is due to the presence of bitter compounds in gotu kola.

Adaptogenic: Gotu kola tea has adaptogenic properties, which means it can help the body cope with stress and maintain balance. This is significant for heartburn as chronic stress can exacerbate heartburn symptoms; thus, gotu kola tea can help manage heartburn by promoting overall well-being and reducing stress-related symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of triterpenoid saponins in gotu kola.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried gotu kola leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool slightly for 1-2 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the gotu kola leaves and let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain out the gotu kola leaves.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for heartburn relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal teas for heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Stems: Gotu kola stems are used in teas for heartburn because they contain compounds that may help to reduce stomach acid and promote digestion.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of gotu kola are used in medicinal teas for heartburn as they contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects.
Flowers: Gotu kola flowers are sometimes included in teas for heartburn because they may help to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can alleviate the symptoms of heartburn. The tea is also known to have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Additionally, German chamomile tea contains certain compounds that can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve digestion and reduce the production of stomach acid, further reducing the risk of heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Spasmolytic: The tea's spasmolytic properties, due to its content of volatile oils like chamazulene, help relax the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, preventing acid reflux and heartburn.
Mucous membrane protection: German chamomile tea contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective film on mucous membranes, helping to soothe and protect the esophageal lining from acid damage.
Antioxidant: The tea's antioxidant properties, provided by flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds, help protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative stress, reducing the risk of heartburn.
Digestive aid: German chamomile tea promotes healthy digestion and can help prevent the overproduction of stomach acid, reducing the likelihood of heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the chamomile flowers.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea, then drink it while warm to soothe heartburn.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe and relax the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile contain essential oils, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that can help reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract and alleviate heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with heartburn because it is made with a blend of herbs that are known to soothe the digestive system and reduce acid reflux. The tea contains ingredients such as licorice root, marshmallow root, and chamomile, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help heal the esophageal lining and reduce irritation. Additionally, the tea can help stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and preventing the occurrence of heartburn. Its natural properties make it a safe and effective alternative to over-the-counter medications for managing heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eureka tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which is important for heartburn as it is often caused by stomach acid irritating the esophagus. The active constituents responsible for this property include catechins and flavonoids.

Mucilage production: Eureka tea stimulates the production of mucilage, a substance that can protect and soothe the lining of the esophagus. This is important for heartburn as it can provide a barrier against stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides and tannins.

Antioxidant: Eureka tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for heartburn as oxidative stress can contribute to the development of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). The active constituents responsible for this property include catechins and flavonoids.

Spasmolytic: Eureka tea has spasmolytic properties, which means it can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract. This is important for heartburn as it can help prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and alkaloids.

Digestive aid: Eureka tea can help improve digestion, which is important for heartburn as poor digestion can contribute to the development of GERD. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils and bitter compounds.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of crushed chamomile flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, add 1 teaspoon of sliced licorice root and 1 teaspoon of grated ginger, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, then stir in 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm and 1 teaspoon of honey, allowing the tea to steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid ingredients.
Drink the tea warm, up to three times a day, as needed for heartburn relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the eureka plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation in the esophagus.
Flowers: The flowers of the eureka plant are used in teas for heartburn because they contain mucilage, which can form a protective coating on the esophageal lining and reduce discomfort.
Seeds: Eureka plant seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to aid in digestion, making them a valuable addition to teas for heartburn relief.
Stems: The stems of the eureka plant contain compounds that can help regulate acid production in the stomach, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the eureka plant are used in teas for heartburn due to their ability to soothe irritated tissue and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat heartburn with herbal teas is by choosing teas with natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. Chamomile, ginger, and licorice root teas are excellent options. Chamomile can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, while ginger helps speed up digestion and neutralizes stomach acids. Licorice root tea coats the esophagus and stomach lining, protecting it from acid. Drink these teas after meals and before bedtime for optimal relief. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider if heartburn persists or worsens.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heartburn that are treated with herbal teas include acid reflux, indigestion, and stomach cramps. Herbal teas such as chamomile, ginger, and peppermint are known for their soothing properties and can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach. Licorice root tea can also help heal the stomach lining and reduce acid production. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas as a treatment, especially for those with underlying medical conditions or taking medications.
